queries: The paper shows that the SA embedding step is the bottleneck for canonical text-MIS recovery, not the QPU. If you had access to a 3D-capable neutral-atom processor tomorrow, which texts in the corpus would benefit most and what canonical recovery ratios would you expect based on the emulator results?
Compare the QPU performance on exact-UDG engineered texts vs. SA-embedded natural texts. Why does the paper argue that the embedding step, not the hardware, limits literary application?

The paper mentions that per-atom local detuning and ancilla-augmented embedding are remedies for hardware-boundary cases like Eighty-One Faces. Explain why these would help and what hardware changes they require.

probes.question: Why is the SA embedding step the bottleneck for canonical text-MIS recovery, not the QPU hardware? What limits 2D SA recall at N>=30?
Why does 3D embedding dramatically improve recall over 2D for Boethius? What geometric constraint does 2D-UDG impose?
Why does the 2D-UDG kissing number limit SA recall? How does bilayer embedding bypass this constraint?
What is the 3D recall value for Boethius (N=72)?
